item.title: Fed Seeks Details on US Banks’ Exposure to Private Credit Firms
item.description: The Federal Reserve is asking major US banks for details about their exposure to private credit following a surge in redemptions from the funds and a rise in troubled loans in the industry, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: PJM Targets 15 Gigawatts of New Power for Data Center Boom
item.description: PJM Interconnection LLC is seeking 15 gigawatts of new power supplies in an emergency proposal to address potential electricity shortages stemming from the boom in artificial intelligence.

item.title: It’s Not Just Oil. A Blocked Hormuz Risks Hiking the Price of Nearly Everything You Buy
item.description: As war in Iran crimps global oil supplies, it is hitting a range of other commodities and promising to remake global supply chains.

item.title: Blackstone Files for IPO of Data Center Acquisition Firm
item.description: Blackstone Inc. filed for an initial public offering of a new data-center acquisition vehicle that plans to buy already-built and leased properties benefiting from the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Top Gulf Aluminum Maker Declares Force Majeure on Some Contracts
item.description: Emirates Global Aluminium, the Middle East’s top producer of the metal, invoked force majeure clauses to suspend at least some deliveries after one of its smelters was put out of action by an Iranian attack.

item.title: Mining Executives Accused of Hijacking Brazil Lithium Claims
item.description: Executives at Canadian minerals explorer Emerita Resources Corp. have been accused by Ontario’s securities regulator of diverting the firm’s lithium project rights to a new company they controlled.

item.title: Wall Street Strategists Wrestle With War’s Toll on 2026 Outlook
item.description: A fragile Middle East ceasefire sent traders rushing back into risk assets this week. Yet Wall Street strategists warn that the war has already scarred inflation, energy supplies and the Federal Reserve’s ability to act — damage no truce can quickly undo.

item.title: Wall Street Banks Try Out Anthropic’s Mythos as US Urges Testing
item.description: Wall Street banks are starting to test Anthropic PBC’s Mythos model internally as Trump administration officials encourage them to use it to detect vulnerabilities.

item.title: Colombia Strikes Back at Ecuador With 100% Reciprocal Tariffs
item.description: Colombia will impose 100% tariffs on imports from Ecuador, deepening trade and diplomatic tensions between Presidents Gustavo Petro and Daniel Noboa.

item.title: Tariff Refund Tool Will Go Live on April 20, US Customs Says
item.description: Importers seeking tariff refunds will be able to begin filing their requests on April 20, US Customs and Border Protection said Friday.

item.title: Harvard’s Kreiman Seeks $100 Million to Build AI Memory Tech
item.description: A new artificial intelligence lab spun out of Harvard University is in talks with investors to raise about $100 million, according to people familiar with the matter, to pursue a mission that sounds like science fiction: “a world where humans can remember everything.”

item.title: NYC’s Car Capital Stung by High Gas Prices, GOP Lawmaker Says
item.description: Motorists in Staten Island, the borough with the highest percentage of car ownership in New York City, are getting hit by soaring gas prices resulting from the US war in Iran, Congresswoman Nicole Malliotakis said.

item.title: BlackRock and State Street Are Coming For Invesco’s Crown Jewel
item.description: Nasdaq loosens the ETF reins on its tech-filled index

item.title: Rio Is Said to Draw Interest for Critical Mineral Assets in California
item.description: Rio Tinto Group has drawn interest from more than a dozen potential bidders for its US assets that produce the critical mineral boron, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Bank of Canada, Major Lenders Meet on Anthropic AI Cyber Risk
item.description: The Bank of Canada and the country’s major banks and financial firms met Friday to discuss cybersecurity risks raised by Anthropic PBC’s latest artificial intelligence model.

item.title: Winklevoss Twins’ $330 Million Gemini Loans in Focus After Rout
item.description: Gemini Space Station Inc. has lost more than half its market value this year, cut 30% of its workforce and retreated from major overseas markets as the crypto exchange struggles financially.

item.title: Organic Juice Maker Suja Files for US IPO Showing Rising Sales
item.description: Suja Life Inc., a maker of organic cold-pressed juices and other nutritional beverages, filed for a US initial public offering, disclosing 26% growth in sales last year.

item.title: Wall Street Seizes on Private Credit Fears With New Way to Short
item.description: Wall Street is debuting a new product that will give investors a way to wager against private credit.

item.title: Why Officials Are So Worried About Mythos, Anthropic’s New AI
item.description: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell summoned Wall Street leaders to give them an urgent warning: an artificial intelligence tool from Anthropic PBC marks the beginning of a new era of cybersecurity.

item.title: Goldman Traders See Record Fast-Money Heading Into US Stocks
item.description: For equity bulls searching for the next catalyst to drive the market higher, Goldman Sachs Group Inc. traders have a message: watch for record buying from algo-driven funds.

item.title: Big US Banks Set to Temper Bond Sales After Strong 2026 Debut
item.description: The six largest US banks are expected to issue fewer bonds this quarter after the busiest start of a year on record.

item.title: Boaz Weinstein Scores Win After UK Trust Backs SpaceX Proposal
item.description: A Baillie Gifford & Co. trust that placed an early bet on SpaceX recommended a proposal backed by activist investor Boaz Weinstein after losing a key shareholder vote.

item.title: Senators Push CFTC to Probe Oil Trading Before Trump Iran News
item.description: Two Democratic US Senators pressed the federal derivatives regulator to investigate suspicious oil futures trading in the lead up to President Donald Trump making two major announcements about military operations in Iran.

item.title: Dallas Stars CEO Sees Rising Team Values on PE, Live Sports Boom
item.description: Private equity interest and the rising popularity of live sports are poised to extend an advance in National Hockey League team valuations after years of strong gains, said Dallas Stars Chief Executive Officer Brad Alberts.

item.title: In a Brutal Video-Game Industry, Two Indie Developers Are Teaming Up
item.description: The makers of the acclaimed Slay the Princess are funding the next game from the maker of the acclaimed 1000xResist

item.title: When Economists and Consumers Agree on Inflation
item.description: There’s no stripping out the effect that surging gasoline prices is having on the US cost of living.

item.title: Raízen Creditors to Demand Management Changes in Counteroffer
item.description: Raízen SA and its creditors are working on a new restructuring proposal for the company’s 65 billion reais ($12.5 billion) debt load following a week of high-stakes meetings in New York, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: France to Nearly Double Fiscal Support for Switch to Electric Power By 2030
item.description: French Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu said the government will divert investment to help households and businesses switch to electric power instead of handing out short-term fuel aid after oil prices surged on the Iran war.

item.title: The Inflationary Pressures Are Still Building
item.description: And consumer sentiment is plungin.

item.title: DeepSeek Looks for Data Center Engineers in Inner Mongolia
item.description: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek is advertising two data center positions in Inner Mongolia, where the company reportedly is relying on banned Nvidia Corp.’s Blackwell chips.
